title: Carolus dukei
intro: The Carolus dukei plant, also known as the Duke of York's pine or Duke of York's fir, is a type of coniferous tree native to the Himalayas. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Carolus dukei include essential oils, terpenes, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and essential oil extracts, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
